title: Combining Conditions
description: Master logical operators to combine multiple conditions - make smarter decisions with AND, OR, and NOT.

phases.title: The AND Operator
phases.description: Learning to check multiple conditions at once

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: What if you need BOTH conditions to be true? That's where the 'and' operator comes in!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So... like checking if I have energy AND seeds at the same time?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! With 'and', both conditions must be True for the whole thing to be True. If either is False, the result is False.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: That's useful! No more nested ifs just to check two things!

objectives.description: Use 'and' operator to combine two conditions

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Perfect! Remember: with 'and', ALL conditions must be True.

phases.title: The OR Operator
phases.description: When any condition being true is enough

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Now let's learn 'or'! This operator returns True if AT LEAST ONE condition is True.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So if I want to plant when I have wheat seeds OR corn seeds, I can use 'or'?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Perfect example! The condition is True if you have wheat seeds, corn seeds, or both!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: And it's only False if NONE of the conditions are True?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly right! All conditions must be False for 'or' to return False.

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: So 'and' needs ALL true, 'or' needs ANY true!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: The 'not' operator flips a boolean value. True becomes False, and False becomes True!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So 'not True' equals False?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! And 'not False' equals True. It's super useful when you want to check if something is NOT the case.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Like checking if my inventory is NOT empty?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Perfect! 'not' helps make your code more readable by expressing negative conditions clearly.

objectives.description: Use 'not' operator to invert a condition

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Great! 'not' is simple but powerful. It makes negative checks much clearer!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Now the fun part! You can combine 'and', 'or', and 'not' in the same condition!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: Wait, like 'if energy > 10 and (has_seeds or has_water)'?

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Exactly! Use parentheses to group conditions, just like math. They control which operations happen first!

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: So I can create really complex decision-making!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Excellent! Remember: 'and' and 'or' have different priorities, so use parentheses to be clear!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Final challenge! Create a smart farming system using logical operators to make complex decisions.

preDialogues.name: Qubit
preDialogues.content: I'll check multiple conditions efficiently!

preDialogues.name: Maru
preDialogues.content: Your goal: harvest 5 wheat crops using logical operators to manage planting and harvesting. Use 'and', 'or', or 'not' to create smart conditions!

objectives.description: Harvest 5 wheat crops using logical operators

postDialogues.name: Qubit
postDialogues.content: My code is so much cleaner with logical operators!

postDialogues.name: Maru
postDialogues.content: Fantastic! You've mastered logical operators. Now you can build truly intelligent systems! Ready for the next challenge?
